key_insight: Mixing training data across datasets by class substantially improves breast MRI tumor classification generalization by reducing dataset-origin bias.
clinical_relevance: For multi-institution breast MRI use, models trained on a single source can look strong internally yet fail externally, so dataset diversity is critical for reliable deployment.
method: Train EfficientNet-B3 and WaveViT-Small on Duke Breast Cancer MRI and fastMRI
Evaluate only on the independent multi-center MAMA-MIA cohort
Compare a confounded setup versus class-wise mixed training with patient-level splitting and leakage controls
evidence_signal: In the confounded setting, external accuracy on MAMA-MIA was near chance (0.5048–0.5265), but class-wise dataset mixing raised performance to 0.8463/0.8625 for WaveViT-Small and 0.8884/0.8994 for EfficientNet-B3.
watch_item: The gains depend on careful leakage control and balanced cross-dataset class mixing, so results may not transfer if dataset origin remains correlated with label.
paper_title: Cross-Dataset Generalization in Breast MRI Tumor Classification via Class-Wise Dataset Mixing
